programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Dance: K-Pop with Caregiver
programs.description: This instructional dance program is based on K-pop. K-pop is a music genre that stands for Korean Pop. K-pop combines different styles and elements of music and dance together. Dance: K-pop may include choreographed dance moves from hip hop, jazz, rock, gospel, R&B, reggae, electronic dance, folk, country, and classical on top of its traditional Korean music roots. A caregiver must actively particiapte in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: K-Pop with Caregiver
programs.description: This instructional dance program is based on K-pop. K-pop is a music genre that stands for Korean Pop. K-pop combines different styles and elements of music and dance together. Dance: K-pop may include choreographed dance moves from hip hop, jazz, rock, gospel, R&B, reggae, electronic dance, folk, country, and classical on top of its traditional Korean music roots. A caregiver must actively particiapte in this activity.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Rookie Patrol
programs.description: The Rookie Patrol program focuses on performance with a 100-metre timed swim and 350-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on assessing conscious victims, treatment of bleeding, and contacting Emergency Medical Services (EMS).Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Star Patrol
programs.description: The Star Patrol program focuses on performance with a 300-metre timed swim and 600-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on treatment of bone and joint injuries as well as responding to respiratory emergencies including asthma and allergic reactions.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Ranger Patrol.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter

programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Rookie Patrol
programs.description: The Rookie Patrol program focuses on performance with a 100-metre timed swim and 350-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on assessing conscious victims, treatment of bleeding, and contacting Emergency Medical Services (EMS).Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Science: Scientific Adventures
programs.description: Jump into the adventures of science. Here, stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play and the use of your imagination will help you to explore science mysteries.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Star Patrol
programs.description: The Star Patrol program focuses on performance with a 300-metre timed swim and 600-metre workouts. It also provides instruction on treatment of bone and joint injuries as well as responding to respiratory emergencies including asthma and allergic reactions.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Ranger Patrol.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting: Landscape
programs.description: Landscapes and nature inspire this drawing and painting activity. Using pencil, charcoal, ink and paints, you will learn to render landscapes and how to incorporate perspective. Where weather allows you will participate in outdoor sketching

programs.name: Drawing From the Round
programs.description: Explore drawing basics including portraiture through working with casts  and photographs to improve your drawing. Get hands-on with casts and  photographs as you nail down proportions, head layout and facial feature  placement. Use light logic to bring surface forms to life through a mix of  lectures, demo drawings, and plenty of drawing time in class. Experiment with  one graphite and one charcoal method on different types of paper, to create two  take home projects. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Drawing Fundamentals
programs.description: This short course starts by teaching how to properly hold and control your pencil to drawing smooth, clean lines and curves that improves hand dexterity to learning the measuring skills that every artist needs. You'll discover angles and distances to make your drawings accurate and proportional. By the end of this course, you'll have a firm grasp of how to measure what you see to draw anything accurately.

programs.name: Drawing in the Garden
programs.description: Using dry media like pencil, pencil crayon or charcoal, explore the many varieties of plants and flowers around the Clark Centre. Instruction on basic drawing technique such as shading, composition and line. Learn to see like an artist using observations and fill your sketchbook with unique and beautiful pieces. Portions of this class will take place out doors depending on weather. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Drawing: Portrait
programs.description: Portraits bring personality to paper. With basic instruction on how to draw your subject’s face and head, learn how to capture a subjects character in a variety of mediums such as pencil, charcoal, pastel and ink.

programs.name: Drawing: Portrait
programs.description: Portraits bring personality to paper. With basic instruction on how to draw your subject’s face and head, learn how to capture a subjects character in a variety of mediums such as pencil, charcoal, pastel and ink.

programs.name: Eco Printing & Dyes
programs.description: Participants will learn this natural dyeing technique that uses leaves, flowers and other plant materials to create patterns and colours on fabric or paper. You'll be guided through the eco-printing process set-by-step - from selecting and arranging plant materials to bundling, steaming and revealing the final designs. Along the way, you'll explore how different plants release natural pigments creating organic patterns, subtle colour variations and unexpected textures. The result is a unique, one-of-a-kind print that captures the beauty of nature. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Candidates will learn first-aid principles and the emergency treatment of injuries. This course teaches victim assessment, CPR, choking and how to respond to external bleeding, heart attack, stroke, wounds and burns.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Etobicoke York Inclusive Request
programs.description: Our inclusive services are designed for participants who may require additional assistance to participate in recreation activities. Inclusive staff assist participants by providing modifications in program instruction, movement, equipment and communication. They also promote and encourage socialization amongst peers. Assignments of Inclusive staff are based on individual application and availability.As this is only a request, participants who waitlist for this activity will be contacted by Adapted and Inclusive Recreation Services for follow-up.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Etobicoke York Inclusive Request
programs.description: Our inclusive services are designed for participants who may require additional assistance to participate in recreation activities. Inclusive staff assist participants by providing modifications in program instruction, movement, equipment and communication. They also promote and encourage socialization amongst peers. Assignments of Inclusive staff are based on individual application and availability.As this is only a request, participants who waitlist for this activity will be contacted by Adapted and Inclusive Recreation Services for follow-up.

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.
